data.body:
=== ROOT-CAUSE INVESTIGATION (rebar-debug protocol) — CONCLUDED ===

Symptom: the completion verifier marks "verified by a test" acceptance criteria UNMET, claiming the test does not exist, when it does — non-deterministically (ad9f flipped FAIL(2 unmet)→PASS(0 unmet) on identical code minutes apart; 4de6 blocked claiming no HEAD-moves test though one exists).

Hypotheses tested (all experiments read-only / no LLM except the final trace):
1. STRUCTURAL — verifier's read-root excludes tests/ → REFUTED. Materialized the attested snapshot at origin/main (git read-tree + checkout-index --all): tests/ present, the exact test file present, grep inside the snapshot finds test_default_close_pins_head_across_a_concurrent_commit.
2. --ref REGRESSION (our 80af/4de6 concrete-SHA change altered the read-root) → REFUTED. resolve_gate_handle("HEAD") vs resolve_gate_handle(<sha>) yield the IDENTICAL snapshot (same path, 2116 files, tests present in both). Passing a concrete SHA vs the string "HEAD" does not change what the verifier reads.
3. TOOL/CONTEXT gap (skip_dir excludes tests, scan cap truncates, no search tool) → REFUTED. Agent HAS search_files (recursive os.walk) + list_directory; "tests" not in _NOISE_DIRS; _SCAN_MAX_FILES=5000 > 2116; git-ls-files tracked-filter is inert on the snapshot (no .git). Calling the agent's ACTUAL search_files on the real snapshot: search_files("4de6","tests") RETURNS the test (3 hits); "concurrent_commit"/"racing_verify" also hit.
4. UNPINNED TEMPERATURE as THE cause → REFUTED as root cause (my earlier over-claim, corrected on challenge). Temperature only modulates which query-strategy the agent lands on; it is not the mechanism.

PROVEN ROOT CAUSE (instrumented run, 1 billable verifier call, tool-call trace captured): the verifier's test-existence judgment depends on the agent's VARIABLE, FALLIBLE free-form search. In the traced run the agent issued 33 search_files calls; search_files(query='4de6', path='tests') → returned the 4de6 test (HIT), so it credited that criterion — BUT it also issued REGEX/SEMANTIC queries against the LITERAL case-sensitive substring tool: query='verify.*sign window', 'drift.*verify.*sign', 'moves HEAD' → all (no matches) (the '.*' is taken literally; those exact substrings aren't in the file). When a run happens to search by semantic phrases/regex instead of the ticket-id/exact-symbol, every query no-matches and the agent concludes "no such test exists." That variance (not file access, not --ref, not primarily temperature) is the defect. Changeable artifacts: the completion-verifier prompt (never tells the agent search_files is literal-substring, nor to search by ticket-id/symbol / list_directory before concluding absence) and the diagnosability gap (sidecar didn't record verify-sha, so drift couldn't be proven on identical input).

FIXES LANDED (this change):
- reviewers/completion_verifier.md: guidance that search_files is LITERAL case-sensitive substring (not regex/glob); locate tests by ticket-id + exact symbol + list_directory; never conclude absence from failed semantic/regex searches. (Directly addresses the proven mechanism.)
- completion.py _verify_completion_inner: pin temperature 0 by default (variance mitigation; explicit REBAR_LLM_TEMPERATURE wins), matching the plan-review Pass-2 verifier's greedy pin.
- completion_sidecar.build_payload: record verified_at_sha + trace_id on BOTH PASS and FAIL records (diagnosability — future drift is provably on identical input).
Tests: tests/unit/test_completion_verifier_diag_e458.py (RED→GREEN, mutation-checked) for the two deterministic code fixes; the prompt fix is an LLM-surface change (behavioral, not unit-tested).
